channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ATOM clarifies it will not join the UniTeam coalition
item.description: In a recent clarification, the August Twenty-One Movement (ATOM) confirmed that it will not form a coalition with the so-called 'UniTeam' or a 'uni-yellow' alliance. This statement comes amidst ongoin...

item.title: RCMP investigates disappearance of Iranian activist in Burnaby
item.description: The B.C. RCMP has initiated a homicide investigation into the disappearance of Masood Masjoody, an Iranian activist and former math instructor at Simon Fraser University, who went missing in early Feb...

item.title: Australian government bans Hizb ut-Tahrir under new hate speech legislation
item.description: The Australian government has officially banned the Islamist organisation Hizb ut-Tahrir, making it a criminal offence to be a member or provide financial support to the group. This move comes under n...

item.title: Kenya Urged to Intensify Preparations for 2026 Women's Africa Cup of Nations
item.description: The editorial calls on Kenyan football authorities to thoroughly prepare the Harambee Starlets for the upcoming Women's Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) in 2026. It stresses that after qualifying, the t...

item.title: DA refuses to remove controversial billboard depicting Gauteng premier Lesufi
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) has refused to comply with the African National Congress’s (ANC) demand to remove a billboard in Mamelodi featuring an AI-generated image of Gauteng premier Panyaza Lesufi...

item.title: US-Israel Bombing Campaign on Iran Enters Seventh Day with Over 1,200 Deaths and Regional Escalation
item.description: The ongoing war between the United States, Israel, and Iran has reached its seventh day, with heavy bombing raids causing widespread destruction across Iranian cities including Tehran. According to th...

item.title: Minister Parks Tau Opposes Tongaat Hulett Liquidation to Avoid Jobs Bloodbath and Sugar Industry Collapse
item.description: Trade and Industry Minister Parks Tau has filed an affidavit in the Durban High Court strongly opposing the provisional liquidation of sugar producer Tongaat Hulett. He argues that liquidation would c...

item.title: The role of informal recyclers in Pakistan’s plastic waste management
item.description: In Pakistan, almost half of the country’s waste is managed by informal recyclers, many of them children, who work under unsafe and unregulated conditions. Karachi alone has an estimated 40,000 waste p...

item.title: Amit Shah Announces Goal to Eliminate Naxal Influence by March End
item.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah, during the 57th raising day celebrations of the Central Industrial Security Force (CISF) at Mundali in Cuttack, Odisha, praised the security forces for their efforts in ...

item.title: Carney supports removing former Prince Andrew from royal succession
item.description: During a visit to the Indo-Pacific region, Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney expressed that he personally believes former Prince Andrew should be removed from the royal line of succession. Carney ci...

item.title: Family Holds Symbolic Burial for Kenyan Soldier Lost in Russia-Ukraine Conflict
item.description: On Saturday, March 7, several local newspapers reported on a series of events shaping Kenya’s current affairs. The most emotional coverage came from Kiambu County, where the family of 33-year-old Jame...

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard denies espionage suspect’s link to organization
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) has clarified that the individual suspected of being involved in an alleged Chinese espionage operation in the country is not a member of its organization. The PCG iss...

item.title: Petrol and Diesel Prices Raised by Rs55 Per Litre Amid Global Tensions
item.description: The Government of Pakistan announced a substantial increase of Rs55 per litre in the prices of petrol and high-speed diesel, effective from 12am on Saturday. The ex-depot rate for petrol is now Rs321....

item.title: UK immigration officers and associates face trial over alleged collaboration with Chinese authorities
item.description: Two former UK immigration officers are on trial following allegations that they assisted Chinese authorities by forcing entry into a flat in Pontefract. Chi Leung 'Peter' Wai and the late Matthew Tric...

item.title: Noida International Airport Receives Operational Licence, Preparations Underway
item.description: The Noida International Airport, situated in Jewar, Uttar Pradesh, has recently been granted a crucial licence allowing it to commence its operations in the near future. This licence is a significant ...

item.title: Zanzibar Farmers Embrace Agroecology for Sustainable Growth
item.description: Zanzibar, long famous for its cloves, is now becoming a model for sustainable agriculture through agroecology. Farmers are increasingly moving away from conventional methods and embracing practices th...

item.title: Herzog's ASIO briefing during Australia visit questioned amid claims of ceremonial role
item.description: Israeli President Isaac Herzog visited Australia in February 2026, following a deadly antisemitic terror attack at Bondi Beach in December 2025 that killed 16 people during a Hanukkah event. The Austr...

item.title: Determining the Composition of Asteroids and Its Implications for Planetary Defence
item.description: Asteroids are remnants from the early Solar System, acting as time capsules that preserve the building blocks of planets. Understanding their composition is crucial not only for scientific curiosity b...

item.title: KP and Federal Govt Join Hands to Launch Peshawar Valley Railway Project
item.description: PESHAWAR: The Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KP) government has announced its plan to collaborate with the federal government for the launch of the Peshawar Valley Railway line. This ambitious project aims to im...

item.title: NCW to Conduct Week-Long Public Hearings in 500 Districts to Address Women’s Complaints
item.description: The National Commission for Women (NCW), in coordination with state women’s commissions, is set to conduct a series of 'Mahila Jan Sunwais' or public hearings across India, spanning 500 districts in 2...
